    Interdisciplinary Quantum Materials at National Taiwan University

    We develop innovative computational methods and software to understand, design, and discover next-generation quantum materials by integrating solid-state physics, first-principles calculations, high-throughput screening, and machine learning.

    Members

    Principal Investigator

    Nguyen Tuan Hung

    • 8/2025 - Present: Assistant Professor (PI), MSE & GUPS, National Taiwan University, Taiwan
    • 8/2025 - Present: Newly Hired Exceptional Talent, National Taiwan University, Taiwan
    • 6/2025 - Present: Adjunct Professor (PI), HCMUS, Vietnam National University-Ho Chi Minh City, Vietnam
    • 4/2019 - 7/2025: Assistant Professor (PI), FRIS, Tohoku University, Japan
    • 6/2021 - 7/2025: Prominent Research Fellow, Tohoku University, Japan
    • 8/2023 - 5/2024: Visiting Scholar, NSE, Massachusetts Institute of Technology, USA
    • 2/2023 - 3/2024: Visiting Scholar, NSE, Massachusetts Institute of Technology, USA
    • 3/2018 - 3/2019: JSPS Research Fellowships for Young Scientists, Japan Society for the Promotion of Science, Japan

    Contact: nguyenth@ntu.edu.tw

    Current Members

    La Thuan Phuoc

    La Thuan Phuoc is Ph.D. student at Vietnam National University-Ho Chi Minh City, Vietnam. He is studying the DFT calculations for the materials science.

    Lakhvir Singh

    Lakhvir Singh is master student at National Taiwan University, Taiwan. He is studying and developing the AI and machine learning for the energy materials.

    Quach Thi Thai Binh

    Quach Thi Thai Binh is master student at Vietnam National University-Ho Chi Minh City, Vietnam. She is studying and developing the AI and machine learning for the materials science.

    Nguyen Tran Gia Bao

    Nguyen Tran Gia Bao is undergraduate student at Vietnam National University-Ho Chi Minh City, Vietnam. He is studying and developing the solid-state physics and DFT method/code for the energy transport.

    Alumni

    Dr. Pratama Fenda Rizky

    Pratama Fenda Rizky received a Ph.D. from Tohoku University in September 2021. He joined the group as a post-doc from 2021 to 2022 and study the thermoelectric properties of the 3D materials. He now is working as a researcher at The International Institute for Sustainability with Knotted Chiral Meta Matter, Hiroshima University, Japan.
